name: TEXTRON INC
business: Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that manufactures and sells aircraft (business jets, turboprops, military trainers, piston engines), helicopters and tiltrotor aircraft, defense systems (electronic systems, marine craft, unmanned aircraft, armored vehicles), industrial products (fuel systems, specialized vehicles), and provides financing for aircraft purchases. Its customers include the U.S. Government, fractional aircraft businesses, charter operators, fleet operators, corporate aviation, individual buyers, training schools, airlines, and military and government operators worldwide.
sellsTo: special mission, military and government operators

exposures.sentence: Our contracts with the U.S. Government, including contracts under the U.S. Government-sponsored foreign military sales program, generated approximately 27% of our consolidated revenues in 2025, primarily in our Bell and Textron Systems segments.

Kautex also offers lightweight, composite Pentatonic battery systems, which include enclosures, underbody protection and thermal management systems, for use in electric vehicles, from hybrid to full battery-powered.
A significant portion of the products sold by the Textron Specialized Vehicles businesses are powered with lithium batteries, reducing the products impact on the environment.
The Textron eAviation segment has been focused on research and development initiatives related to sustainable aviation solutions and includes Pipistrel, a manufacturer of light aircraft.

Pipistrel offers a family of light aircraft and gliders with both electric and combustion engines, including the Velis Electro, which is the world s first, and currently only, electric aircraft to receive full type certification from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency and from the UK Civil Aviation Authority.

The Textron eAviation segment has also been developing both hybrid and electric propulsion aircraft, including Pipistrel's Nuuva V300, a long-range, large-capacity hybrid-electric vertical takeoff and landing unmanned aircraft.

catalysts.event: Beechcraft Denali expected to enter into service in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Denali is expected to enter into service in 2026.

catalysts.event: Citation M2 Gen3, CJ3 Gen3, CJ4 Gen3 expected to enter into service in 2027
catalysts.sentence: Currently under development, the M2 Gen3, CJ3 Gen3 and CJ4 Gen3 are expected to enter into service in 2027.

catalysts.event: MV-75 program: first prototype tests planned for 2026
catalysts.sentence: This phase includes continued digital modeling, detailed hardware and software design, and fabrication of hardware, as Bell proceeds to critical design review and the first prototype tests planned for 2026.

catalysts.event: Textron eAviation segment realignment effective beginning of 2026 fiscal year
catalysts.sentence: Effective January 4, 2026, the beginning of our 2026 fiscal year, the business activities of the Textron eAviation segment were realigned within Textron's other operating segments resulting in the elimination of the Textron eAviation segment as a separate reporting segment.
